channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Here Are All the K-Pop Albums That Have Topped the Billboard 200: Full List
item.description:
[Intro music plays]
Host: "Welcome to our latest update on the world of K-pop. If you're a fan of the genre, you'll be excited to know that BTS is back on top with their new album ARIRANG. For the third week in a row, ARIRANG has taken the No. 1 spot on the all-genre Billboard 200 chart, a feat that's never been achieved by a K-pop album or EP before."
[Soft music plays in the background]
Host: "This marks the 23rd time a K-pop act has reached No. 1 on the Billboard 200 chart in less than eight years. The first K-pop album to achieve this was BTS' Love Yourself: Tear back in June 2018. Since then, we've seen a surge in K-pop popularity, with groups like Stray Kids, ATEEZ, and BLACKPINK making waves on the chart."
[Music transitions to a more upbeat tone]
Host: "Speaking of Stray Kids, they're currently leading the pack with eight No. 1 titles on the Billboard 200. BTS is close behind with seven No. 1s, and ATEEZ has two. Other notable groups like SuperM, BLACKPINK, and TOMORROW X TOGETHER have also reached No. 1 on the chart. BLACKPINK, in fact, was the first female K-pop act to land a No. 1 album on the Billboard 200 with their album Born Pink in 2022."
[Music returns to a softer tone]
Host: "It's interesting to note that K-pop groups often have large ensembles. TWICE, for example, has nine members, while Stray Kids and ATEEZ have eight. BTS and SuperM have seven members each. And did you know that no K-pop solo artists, duos, or trios have topped the Billboard 200 chart yet?"
[Music transitions to a more energetic tone]
Host: "K-pop has been consistently dominating the Billboard 200 chart since 2018, with the exception of 2021. The peak year for K-pop albums on the chart was 2023, with five No. 1 albums. We're excited to see what the future holds for K-pop and which groups will continue to make history on the Billboard 200 chart."
[Outro music plays]
Host: "That's all for today's update on K-pop. Thanks for tuning in, and we'll catch you in the next one."
